items.title: Feraşîn Highlands Thrive as Rich Pastures Boost Livestock and Dairy Output
items.desc: Abundant rainfall has revitalized Northern Kurdistan's Feraşîn Highlands' pastures, improving grazing conditions, increasing milk production, and supporting seasonal livestock farming and traditional cheese and yogurt production across the mountain region.

items.title: Coalition Forces Down Five Explosive Drones Over Erbil, CTD Says
items.desc: According to the CTD, the drones were intercepted between 9:32 a.m. and 9:45 a.m. after being launched from the western part of Erbil province. The agency said no casualties or material damage were reported.

items.title: Cowpeas and Figs Put Taq Taq at the Center of Kurdistan's Harvest Season
items.desc: Peak harvest season in Taq Taq is sending 75 tons of cowpeas to markets each day, while the area's renowned figs and expanding agricultural trade are creating jobs and supporting rural incomes across the Kurdistan Region.

items.title: UN Urges Evacuation of 6,000 Stranded Seafarers in Strait of Hormuz
items.desc: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk urged countries involved in the Middle East conflict, along with shipowners and other stakeholders, to take immediate action to protect stranded crews.

items.title: Venice Film Festival Selects Halkawt Mustafa's Latest Kurdish Feature
items.desc: Kurdish filmmaker Halkawt Mustafa's latest feature will make its world premiere at the Venice International Film Festival, marking a significant milestone for Kurdish cinema through an internationally backed production inspired by the experiences of women who fought ISIS.

items.title: Global Coalition downs five drones over Erbil
items.desc: Shafaq News- Erbil The Kurdistan Region’s Counter-Terrorism Directorate reported on Friday that Global Coalition air defenses shot down five explosive drones over Erbil. The drones were launched from west of the province, causing no casualties. Falling debris sparked a fire in dry grass and shrubs, which Erbil Civil Defense teams extinguished. Read more: Who is striking Iraqi Kurdistan? New

items.title: Iraq’s PM al-Zaidi offers Tehran and Washington a corridor, not a battlefield
items.desc: Shafaq News Iraqi Prime Minister Ali Falih al-Zaidi traveled to Tehran on Thursday for a one-day visit that produced railway agreements, a twinning arrangement between the two capitals, and a commitment to draft a comprehensive strategic document. The substance of the visit lay in what was not signed: an Iraqi bid to broker between Washington and Tehran at a moment when both appear willing to let

items.title: Iraqi PM office denies ceasefire mediation report
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad The Media Office of the Iraqi Prime Minister has denied a New York Times report that Prime Minister Ali Al-Zaidi carried a United States ceasefire proposal to Tehran, saying the allegations are "entirely unfounded and bear no relation to reality." Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi separately dismissed reports that Al-Zaidi, during his visit to Tehran, brought a message f

items.title: Farmers seek protection for Najaf rice revival
items.desc: Shafaq News- Najaf Rice farmers in Iraq’s Najaf province have urged the government to help cover production costs after improved water supplies revived fields left largely uncultivated during last year’s drought. In Al-Issa, one of the main rice growing areas in the province’s Kufa district, farmer Abu Amir Al-Issawi told Shafaq News that he planted more than 17.5 hectares with A

items.title: Two missiles found in southern Iraq desert
items.desc: Shafaq News- Al-Muthanna Iraqi security forces on Friday found two small military missiles, including an unexploded cruise missile. A security source told Shafaq News that the missiles had fallen “recently” in the Al-Busayya desert of southern Al-Muthanna province, but did not identify their model, intended target, or the party that launched them. Read more: Erbil Airport remains open

items.title: US hits dozens of countries with new wave of tariffs
items.desc: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February.

items.title: This new bridge aimed to show US-Canada unity but instead stoked divisions
items.desc: A bridge linking Michigan to Ontario is about to open but Canada has disinvited US dignitaries from the ceremony.

items.title: Using a bathroom 20 times a day - life in the middle of US cyclospora outbreak
items.desc: A historic cyclospora outbreak spreading across the US is causing havoc in pockets of Michigan, the hardest hit state.

items.title: Gruesome images in D4vd hearing are 'worst nightmare' for teen girl's family
items.desc: The court hears the singer ordered a body bag, chainsaws and an inflatable pool to dispose of the teen's body.

items.title: US health panel loosens restrictions for controversial peptides popular online
items.desc: The vote marks a win for Health Secretary Robert F Kennedy who has said he's a big fan of the drugs, which haven't been well studied for human use.

items.title: How the Houthis blockade of the Red Sea could affect the global economy
items.desc: NPR's A Martínez asks former U.S. deputy national security adviser Jon Finer what the blockade of the Red Sea by Houthi rebels means for the global economy.

items.title: US allies hit with new tariffs object to Trump's forced labor claims
items.desc: Australia and the European Union were among U.S. trade partners who condemned the tariffs.

items.title: Netflix viewers the world over are binge-watching South African series 'The Polygamist.' Here's why
items.desc: Why is a Zulu-language drama taking the world by storm? The Polygamist mixes sex and scandal with a sharp portrait of modern South Africa.

items.title: Indian activist Wangchuk ends 26-day hunger strike as 'Cockroach' protesters vow to press on
items.desc: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike on Friday that helped galvanize a youth movement that's become one of India's largest protests in recent years, but demonstrators vowed to continue until the education minister resigns.

items.title: Rubio says U.S. is ready to help end war in Ukraine but no quick deal ahead
items.desc: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, who met with his Russian counterpart Sergey Lavrov, said that President Trump was committed to the work "if conditions and factors have changed to make that possible."

items.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
items.desc: The US president says the landmark agreement to help Saudis develop nuclear energy is dependent on it joining the Abraham Accords.

items.title: Trump's nuclear deal with Saudis jettisons longstanding US demands
items.desc: Signing a nuclear pact in the Middle East is a bold move that has drawn criticism in the US and in Israel.

items.title: Houthi attacks raise fears of wider Middle East conflict and more global economic damage
items.desc: The Iran-backed group in Yemen has threatened to target Saudi vessels in a vital shipping route.

items.title: 'What was their crime?': BBC visits Iran school where strike killed 120 children
items.desc: Nearly five months on, grief is still raw as a teacher and a mother recall the horror of the attack in Minab.

items.title: Oil prices hit $100 for the first time since May
items.desc: The price of Brent crude rose more than 6% on Thursday as the war in the Middle East continues to escalate.
